name: African Violet
desc: A soft, powdery violet with a delicate watery quality — warmer and rounder than common violet leaf.

name: Aldehydes
desc: Synthetic molecules producing clean, soapy, waxy brightness — powdery and luminous.

name: Almond
desc: Warm, sweet, slightly creamy nut note. A comforting, slightly powdery sweetness often used in gourmand compositions.

name: Amber
desc: A warm resinous accord of labdanum, benzoin, and vanilla. Rich, golden, slightly powdery.

name: Amberwood
desc: A warm, smooth woody amber accord — softer and less resinous than labdanum, with a clean, slightly sweet quality.

name: Ambrette
desc: Seed of the musk mallow. Musky, slightly nutty, subtly floral — a natural musk substitute.

name: Angelica
desc: Earthy, herbal root note with a slightly musky, bittersweet quality. Bridges green and woody accords.

name: Apple
desc: Fresh, crisp, lightly tart fruit note. Green apple is sharper; red apple sweeter and juicier.

name: Artemisia
desc: Bitter, herbaceous, slightly medicinal. From the wormwood family — dry, aromatic, and untamed.

name: Basil
desc: Fresh, anise-like, slightly sweet herbal note. Bright, aromatic, and unexpectedly elegant.

name: Bay Leaf
desc: Spicy, aromatic, slightly clove-like. Warm and herbal with a distinctive culinary character.

name: Beeswax
desc: Warm, honeyed, lightly floral. The natural wax from honeycombs — golden, slightly sweet, deeply comforting.

name: Benzoin
desc: A sweet, vanilla-like balsamic resin. Warm and powdery with a gentle cinnamon quality. Prized in oriental compositions.

name: Bergamot
desc: Cold-pressed from a sour Italian citrus rind. Bright, clean, slightly floral.

name: Birch
desc: Smoky, tar-like, and leathery. Used in small doses for a cool forest-smoke effect.

name: Bitter Almond
desc: Sharp, intensely almond, slightly medicinal. More forceful than sweet almond — contains a marzipan-like edge.

name: Black Cherry
desc: Dark, jammy, slightly smoky cherry. Deeper and richer than fresh cherry, with a hint of wood and wine.

name: Black Currant
desc: Sharp, tart, slightly catty. The leaf is more vegetal — green and sulfurous.

name: Black Grape
desc: Dark, slightly tannic fruit note. Less sweet than table grape — vinous, slightly fermented, with a wine-like quality.

name: Black Orchid
desc: A rich, dark, exotic floral with spice and chocolate undertones. Opulent and somewhat mysterious.

name: Black Pepper
desc: Sharp, forceful, slightly smoky spice. More assertive and darker than pink pepper — adds a dry, biting heat.

name: Black Tea
desc: Deeper and more tannic than green tea. Slightly smoky, malty, and warm — evokes a strong afternoon brew.

name: Blackberry
desc: Dark, tart, slightly earthy berry. Less sweet than raspberry — more tannic, jammy, and complex.

name: Blackcurrant Leaf
desc: More vegetal and green than the fruit — sharp, slightly sulfurous, intensely natural.

name: Blood Orange
desc: Vibrant, tart citrus with a faint dark berry undertone that distinguishes it from standard orange.

name: Cacao
desc: Raw, earthy chocolate. Less processed than cocoa — slightly bitter and vegetal with a dark richness.

name: Caramel
desc: Sweet, slightly burnt sugar. Warm and indulgent — can range from delicate toffee to dark, almost savory burnt caramel.

name: Cardamom
desc: Spicy, camphoraceous, slightly eucalyptus-like. Warm without being sweet.

name: Casablanca Lily
desc: A full, creamy white lily — opulent, slightly heady, and intensely floral. More lush than standard lily.

name: Cashew
desc: Warm, buttery, slightly sweet nut note. Creamy and mellow with a subtle toasted quality.

name: Cashmeran
desc: A synthetic musk-wood with a soft, cozy quality — slightly spicy, woody, and skin-close. Used to add warmth and diffusion.

name: Cashmere
desc: A soft, warm synthetic textile accord. Smooth, slightly powdery, and skin-like — evoking the feel of fine cashmere.

name: Cedar
desc: Clean, dry, pencil-shaving woody note. Virginia Cedar lighter; Atlas Cedar creamier.

name: Cedar Milk
desc: A creamy, soft cedar variant — milky and smooth rather than dry and sharp.

name: Cedarwood
desc: Deeper and warmer than cedar. Slightly sweet, balsamic, and smooth.

name: Chestnut
desc: Roasted, earthy, slightly sweet. Evokes autumn — warm and nutty with a slightly floury texture.

name: Chinese Pepper
desc: More floral and citrusy than black pepper — lemony, slightly spicy, with a distinctive numbing quality.

name: Cinnamon
desc: Warm, sweet, bark spice. Cassia-type is more forceful; Ceylon cinnamon is softer and more complex.

name: Clary Sage
desc: Herbaceous, slightly sweet and earthy. A more floral, aromatic sage — less medicinal than common sage.

name: Clove
desc: Spicy, sharp, eugenol-forward. Dense and slightly medicinal.

name: Cocoa
desc: Dark, roasted chocolate. Richer and more processed than cacao — warm, sweet, and deeply comforting.

name: Coconut
desc: Sweet, tropical, creamy. Full and round — can be fresh and watery or rich and sunscreen-like depending on treatment.

name: Coconut Milk
desc: Smooth, creamy, and tropical. Softer than fresh coconut — milky, slightly sweet, with an almost skin-like quality.

name: Coconut Water
desc: Light, slightly sweet aquatic note with tropical softness.

name: Coffee
desc: Roasted, bitter, warming. Can range from fresh-ground to dark espresso — rich, aromatic, and unmistakable.

name: Copal Resin
desc: Sacred tree resin burned as incense across Mesoamerica for millennia. Smoky, slightly piney, and deeply ceremonial — lighter than frankincense but with a similar ritual character.

name: Coriander
desc: Warm, slightly citrusy spice with a faintly earthy, dried-herb character. Drier than cardamom.

name: Cumin
desc: Warm, earthy, slightly animalic spice. Adds depth and a somewhat sweaty character that's intensely human and sensual.

name: Cyclamen
desc: Delicate, watery floral with a slightly metallic edge.

name: Cypress
desc: Resinous, dry, slightly smoky. Austere, cool, bitter-green.

name: Cypriol
desc: Smoky, earthy, rootsy wood from cyperus grass (nagarmotha). Used for depth and a slightly animalic smokiness in oriental compositions.

name: Dried Fruits
desc: Concentrated, slightly fermented, plummy sweetness. Prune-like, figgy, and rich — adds complexity to amber and oud compositions.

name: Driftwood
desc: Sun-bleached, salt-worn wood. Dry, slightly marine.

name: Elemi
desc: A bright, piney resin with a lemony, slightly peppery quality. Lighter and more citrusy than frankincense.

name: Eucalyptus
desc: Cool, camphoraceous, medicinal. Fresh and astringent — used sparingly to add a bracing, clean quality.

name: Fig
desc: Ripe fig — sweet, milky, and slightly earthy. Rounder and more fruity than fig leaf or fig wood.

name: Fig Leaf
desc: Sharper and more vegetal than fig fruit — green, slightly tart, milky at the edges.

name: Fig Milk
desc: The milky sap of the fig tree — softly creamy, slightly bitter.

name: Fig Wood
desc: Dry, slightly dusty woody note from the inner branches.

name: Fir Balsam
desc: Resinous, piney, slightly sweet balsam. Evokes cold northern forests — fresh, clean, with a sticky resinous depth.

name: Frankincense
desc: Sacred resin from the Boswellia tree. More piney and citrusy than incense — clean, slightly lemony, and meditative.

name: Freesia
desc: Light, airy, slightly spiced floral. Clean and transparent with a faint citrus edge.

name: Gardenia
desc: Creamy, intensely sweet white tropical flower. Slightly coconut-like, narcotic, and full — one of the most opulent florals.

name: Geranium
desc: Rose-like but sharper — minty, green, slightly metallic.

name: Ginger
desc: Sharp, warm, slightly woody spice. Drier than cardamom.

name: Grapefruit
desc: Bright, bitter-sweet citrus with a slightly waxy quality.

name: Grass
desc: Crisp, fresh, green-cut grass. Clean and natural with a slightly sweet, coumarin-touched quality when dry.

name: Green Fig
desc: Unripe fig — crisp, tart, green. Less creamy than ripe fig.

name: Green Leaves
desc: A generic fresh leaf note — watery, crisp, and green. Adds freshness and natural quality to many compositions.

name: Green Tea
desc: Dry, slightly tannic, clean with subtle grassiness. Cooling.

name: Guaiac Wood
desc: Smoky, slightly rosy, dry resinous wood from South America. Adds a soft, diffuse smokiness to woody compositions.

name: Hay
desc: Dry, sun-warmed, coumarin-tinged. Sweet and slightly floral when dried — evokes warm summer fields.

name: Heliotrope
desc: Sweet, almond-like, slightly powdery floral. Warm and round — a delicate vanilla-floral that bridges gourmand and floral families.

name: Hinoki
desc: Japanese cypress wood. Warm, slightly lemony, and clean.

name: Honey
desc: Warm, sweet, slightly animalic. Natural honey in perfumery has a waxy, slightly beeswax quality — golden and indulgent.

name: Honeysuckle
desc: Nectar-sweet, light, and transparent floral. Clean, luminous, and effortlessly pretty.

name: Hyacinth
desc: Intensely sweet, watery spring floral with a slightly green, powdery edge.

name: Incense
desc: Smoky, resinous, slightly sweet. From burning resins — meditative and ancient.

name: Iris
desc: Powdery, slightly earthy, and cool. From orris root.

name: Iso E Super
desc: A synthetic molecule with a soft, cedar-like, diffuse woody character. Amplifies other notes and adds a quiet resonance.

name: Italian Cypress
desc: Drier and more austere than standard cypress — dark, resinous, slightly medicinal, evoking Mediterranean landscapes.

name: Ivy
desc: Crisp, clean, slightly bitter green. Herbaceous and watery.

name: Ivy Leaf
desc: Cool, slightly metallic green with watery bitterness.

name: Jasmine
desc: Rich, indolic white floral. Slightly animalic and dense.

name: Juniper
desc: Dry, piney, slightly peppery. Northern forests, cold air.

name: Juniper Berry
desc: Fresher and fruitier than juniper wood alone — bright, piney, slightly gin-like. Used to add a bracing, aromatic quality.

name: Labdanum
desc: A dark, resinous gum from cistus plants. Warm, animalic, amber-like.

name: Lavender
desc: Herbaceous, slightly camphorous, softly floral.

name: Lavender Absolute
desc: Richer and more resinous than standard lavender — deeper, slightly sweet, with more complexity and longevity.

name: Leather
desc: A dry, slightly smoky accord evocative of new leather.

name: Lemon
desc: Bright, sharp, clean citrus. One of the most universally used top notes.

name: Lily
desc: Clean, slightly sweet white floral. Softer and less dense than tuberose or gardenia — fresh, dewy, and classic.

name: Lily of the Valley
desc: Cool, delicate white floral with a watery green stem. Clean, classic, and slightly dewy.

name: Lychee
desc: Delicate tropical fruit — sweet, floral, slightly watery. Light and translucent.

name: Magnolia
desc: Fresh, lightly spiced white floral with a hint of citrus.

name: Mandarin
desc: Sweeter and softer than orange — gentle citrus with a slightly floral undertone.

name: Marigold
desc: Warm, slightly herbal floral with a tart, tomato-leaf edge.

name: Marine Accord
desc: A synthetic aquatic freshness evoking sea spray and ocean air. Clean, slightly salty, and airy.

name: Mastic
desc: Piney, aromatic resin from the mastic tree. Slightly sweet, fresh, and resinous — used in Mediterranean perfumery for centuries.

name: Matcha
desc: Earthy, grassy, subtly bitter green tea powder. More vegetal and robust than steeped green tea — deeply Japanese in character.

name: Mate
desc: Dry, slightly smoky, tea-like. From yerba mate.

name: Mimosa
desc: Soft, powdery, honey-like yellow floral. Warm, slightly solar, and gently animalic.

name: Mineral Accord
desc: A clean, dry, stone-like quality. Evoking wet rocks, limestone, or a cool cave — abstract and otherworldly.

name: Mint
desc: Cool, camphoraceous, and sharp. Immediately refreshing.

name: Moss
desc: Damp, earthy, lush forest floor. Distinct from oakmoss — more woodland and less structured, evoking rain on stones.

name: Musk
desc: A broad category of skin-like base notes — clean, animalic, or transparent.

name: Muslin
desc: A soft, clean textile accord — lightly powdery, slightly soapy.

name: Myrrh
desc: Dark, balsamic, slightly medicinal resin. Warmer and earthier than frankincense — animalic, smoky, and ancient.

name: Narcissus
desc: Heady, green-tinged floral — earthy, slightly animalic, and deeply complex.

name: Neroli
desc: Orange blossom distillate — clean, slightly honeyed, gently spicy.

name: Night-Blooming Jasmine
desc: More intense and indolic than standard jasmine — heady, slightly animalic, and intoxicating. Blooms only in darkness.

name: Nutmeg
desc: Warm, slightly woody, mildly sweet spice. Softer than clove — adds a subtle, rounded warmth to oriental compositions.

name: Oakmoss
desc: Damp, mossy, earthy — the defining base note of classic chypre compositions.

name: Orange
desc: Warm, sweet citrus — less tart than lemon. Gentle and solar, it adds brightness without sharpness.

name: Orange Blossom
desc: White floral, honeyed, slightly indolic. More intense and waxy than neroli.

name: Orchid
desc: Sweet, tropical, lightly vanilla-like floral. More abstract and soft than other white florals — clean and slightly powdery.

name: Oud
desc: Resinous heartwood of infected agarwood. Smoky, dark, and complex.

name: Palisander
desc: Brazilian rosewood. Warm, slightly sweet, and smooth.

name: Palo Santo
desc: Sacred South American wood burned as incense for millennia. Sweet, slightly minty, woody smoke — cleansing and deeply spiritual.

name: Paperwhite Accord
desc: A clean, watery, slightly green narcissus-like floral. Crisp and transparent — evoking the freshness of spring paperwhite flowers.

name: Papyrus
desc: Dry, slightly dusty, reedy — evocative of aged paper and river grasses.

name: Patchouli
desc: Dark, earthy, slightly sweet. Improves with age.

name: Peach
desc: Soft, ripe, slightly velvety. A gentle fruity modifier.

name: Pear
desc: Soft, dewy, slightly floral fruit note. Gentler and more watery than apple.

name: Peony
desc: Soft, rosy-fruity floral — lush but clean. Less dense than rose, more transparent.

name: Pepper
desc: Sharp, dry, slightly smoky. Black pepper adds bite; pink fruitier.

name: Petitgrain
desc: From leaves and twigs of the bitter orange tree. Woody, green, slightly floral.

name: Petrichor
desc: The clean, mineral smell of rain falling on dry earth. Earthy, slightly metallic, and deeply evocative of storms and renewal.

name: Pine
desc: Resinous, fresh, slightly camphoraceous. Cold forests.

name: Pine Resin
desc: Sticky, sharp, intensely resinous pine sap. More raw and forceful than pine needles — deeply forest, almost medicinal.

name: Pink Pepper
desc: Fruity, slightly spicy, and rosy. Less harsh than black pepper.

name: Plum
desc: Dark, jammy, slightly fermented. Adds depth to amber compositions.

name: Queen of the Night
desc: A rare nocturnal cactus flower — intensely sweet, creamy, intoxicating.

name: Rain Accord
desc: A dewy, fresh synthetic accord evoking rain. Clean, slightly metallic, and atmospheric — a modern aquatic note.

name: Raspberry
desc: Bright, tart, slightly floral berry. Sweeter and more delicate than blackberry — feminine, effervescent, and fresh.

name: Resin
desc: Warm, sweet, and balsamic tree secretions. Adds richness and longevity.

name: Rhubarb
desc: Crisp, tart, slightly earthy stalk note. Unexpectedly elegant and floral in fine fragrance.

name: Rose
desc: The most iconic floral. From fresh Turkish to dark Bulgarian absolute.

name: Rosemary
desc: Sharp, piney, herbal, slightly camphoraceous. One of the most recognizable aromatic herbs — clean, Mediterranean, and bracing.

name: Rosewood
desc: Sweet, slightly floral woody note with a rose-cedar character.

name: Rum
desc: Warm, slightly sweet, and alcoholic. Adds boozy character to amber.

name: Saffron
desc: Deeply warm, slightly sweet, leathery and honeyed. One of the most expensive naturals in perfumery.

name: Sage
desc: Dry, herbal, slightly dusty and medicinal. Used sparingly to add an aromatic, almost camphoraceous quality.

name: Salt
desc: A clean, mineral, slightly marine quality. Dry rather than aquatic — enhancing other notes with a subtle mineral brightness.

name: Salted Amber
desc: Amber with a mineral sea-salt accord — golden and resinous.

name: Sandalwood
desc: Creamy, warm, slightly milky. Mysore sandalwood is most prized.

name: Sapodilla
desc: A tropical fruit note — sweet, caramel-brown, slightly smoky.

name: Sea Salt
desc: A briny, oceanic saltiness. Adds a marine, slightly ozonic quality — evocative of sea spray and coastal air.

name: Shiso
desc: Japanese herb, clean, slightly minty with anise-like freshness. Used in perfumery to add a distinctly Asian herbal quality.

name: Smoke
desc: A clean, slightly wood-ash smoky quality. Different from incense — drier, more austere, evoking embers and open fires.

name: Spearmint
desc: Sweeter and softer than peppermint — gently herbaceous with a mild, rounded coolness. Elegant rather than toothpaste-forward.

name: Styrax
desc: Sweet, balsamic, slightly smoky resin. Similar to benzoin but warmer and slightly more leathery — adds richness to oriental bases.

name: Suede
desc: A soft, powdery leather accord. Smoother and more diffuse than full leather — skin-close and slightly woody.

name: Tangerine
desc: Sweet, warm citrus — gentler and more golden than lemon, with a soft floral quality.

name: Tea
desc: Dry, slightly tannic, clean. Green tea more vegetal; black tea warmer.

name: Thorns
desc: A sharp, green, slightly metallic note evoking rose thorns and plant stems. Adds a tart, vegetal edge to floral compositions.

name: Thyme
desc: Dry, herbal, slightly medicinal. Aromatic and warm with a woody underpinning.

name: Tiare
desc: Tahitian gardenia (Gardenia taitensis) — intensely sweet, creamy, and tropical. One of the most opulent white florals used in perfumery.

name: Tobacco
desc: Warm, slightly sweet, hay-like. Dry tobacco austere; flower softer.

name: Tobacco Blossom
desc: The soft, sweet flower of the tobacco plant. Far more floral and honeyed than cured tobacco — light, slightly animalic.

name: Tobacco Flower
desc: Warm, floral, sweeter than cured tobacco leaf. A nectar-like quality that softens the harshness of tobacco into a fragrant bloom.

name: Tobacco Leaf
desc: Dry, cured, hay-like. The raw, slightly bitter quality of dried tobacco — earthier and more austere than processed tobacco.

name: Tomato Leaf
desc: Sharp, vegetal, slightly sulfurous green note. Intensely natural and photorealistic — evokes a summer garden at its most vivid.

name: Tonka
desc: Sweet, warm, coumarin-rich. Smells of vanilla, hay, and almond.

name: Tuberose
desc: Dense, creamy, intensely indolic white floral. Heady and complex.

name: Tulip
desc: Clean, slightly waxy, cool spring floral. Delicate and understated — less opulent than rose or tuberose, more transparent.

name: Vanilla
desc: Sweet, warm, universally appealing. Natural absolute is more complex.

name: Vetiver
desc: Earthy, smoky, and woody root note. One of the most versatile base notes.

name: Violet
desc: Powdery, slightly sweet, and delicate. Soft and nostalgic.

name: Violet Leaf
desc: Green, watery, slightly metallic. Sharper than the flower.

name: White Cedar
desc: Lighter and more airy than standard cedar — clean, slightly resinous.

name: White Musk
desc: The cleanest of musks — transparent, soapy, skin-like.

name: White Rose
desc: Cleaner, cooler, and less opulent than red rose. Delicate and slightly dewy — fresh and classical rather than dense and sensual.

name: White Tea
desc: More delicate and floral than green tea. Light, slightly honeyed, and barely-there — the most ethereal of the tea family.

name: Yellow Oleander
desc: A tropical flowering shrub — warm, honeyed floral, slightly waxy.

name: Ylang-Ylang
desc: Intensely sweet tropical floral with banana and custard undertones. Can dominate a blend.

name: Yuzu
desc: Japanese citrus — tart, pithy, complex. Somewhere between grapefruit, lemon, and bergamot.
